Document:
# Plasmamen
[color=#ffa500]Warning! This species is not recommended for new players due to their tendency to self-ignite![/color]
## Mechanics
[color=#ffa500]They breathe plasma[/color], and [color=#ec3a3a]oxygen is highly toxic to them when inhaled.[/color] Being exposed to oxygen [color=#ec3a3a][bold]sets them on fire.[/color][/bold]
To prevent ignition, they wear sealed suits made up of an envirosuit (in the jumpsuit slot) and a [bold]spaceworthy[/bold] envirosuit helmet. Other suits like EVA suits, vacsuits, tacsuits and hardsuits will also seal them from oxygen exposure.
[color=#1e90ff] They don't experience hunger nor thirst. They don't have blood and cannot bleed out. [/color]
Due to their skeleton body, they can consume [color=#1e90ff]Milk[/color] to gain a positive moodlet and slowly heal [color=#1e90ff]Brute[/color], [color=#1e90ff]Heat[/color], [color=#1e90ff]Shock[/color], and [color=#1e90ff]Caustic[/color] damage. They can also consume [color=#8658fc]Plasma [/color]to gain a bigger positive moodlet and [color=#8658fc] heal every damage type faster[/color], except for Cellular damage.
They infuse their [color=yellow]fists[/color] with plasma dust that combusts when they punch, dealing [color=orange]5 Heat[/color] and [color=red]2.25 Blunt[/color] damage. [bold]However[/bold], each successful punch deals [color=orange]1 Heat[/color] damage to their hands.
Plasmamen with the traits [color=yellow][bold]Claws[/bold][/color], [color=yellow][bold]Talons[/bold][/color] or [color=yellow][bold]Natural Weapons Removal[/bold][/color] have less unarmed damage but don't take self-damage from unarmed attacks.
They take [color=#1e90ff]25% less Piercing[/color], [color=#1e90ff]20% less Poison[/color], and [color=#1e90ff]15% less Slash[/color], and are [color=#8658fc]immune to Radiation and Cold[/color] damage, but take [color=#ffa500]50% more Blunt[/color] and [color=#ffa500]50% more Heat[/color] damage.
Due to their [color=#8658fc]Cold immunity[/color], they can live in cold environments without the need of a winter coat.
They have a slightly [color=#ec3a3a]lower density than the average species[/color]. This results in weaknesses such as but not limited to: being slightly more [color=#ec3a3a]vulnerable to being shoved[/color], [color=#ec3a3a]picked up[/color], and having a [color=#ec3a3a]harder time pulling heavy objects[/color].
## Equipment
All Plasmamen start with an envirosuit, an envirosuit helmet, and a plasma internals tank.
Their envirosuits contain a [color=#1e90ff]self-extinguisher[/color] with limited charges, which they can activate when they are on fire and wearing their envirosuit helmet. The self-extinguisher needs to recharge between uses.
Cartridge refills of the envirosuit's self-extinguisher can be bought in [bold]Loadouts[/bold], or printed at the medical techfab after research. A refill makes a recharging self-extinguisher immediately usable again.
Their envirosuit helmets are [bold]spaceworthy[/bold] and provide welding protection. The helmet's visor can be lit up like a flashlight, using the inserted power cell as a battery. They cannot eat nor drink without taking off their envirosuit helmet.
The plasma internals tank lasts roughly an hour before needing to be replaced or refilled.
## Medical Treatment
Before performing surgery on a Plasmaman, their envirosuit needs to be unequipped first, which is disastrous in an oxygenated environment.
To get around this, the Plasmaman can be buckled to a [bold]stasis bed[/bold] before removing their envirosuit to prevent them from self-igniting throughout the surgical procedure.
Their envirosuit helmets prevents them from eating pills, but their envirosuits allow chemicals to be injected through them.
